subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

files.fileName: Business Management IA - Bhargav Naig + Supporting Documents.pdf

strengths: Clear identification and framing of âchangeâ as the key concept throughout the investigation
Sound linkage of the 2022 ownership transfer and 2018 mission-statement shift to competitive outcomes
Accurate organizational details (Purpose Trust, Holdfast Collective, revenue figures) that support the concept
Consistent analytical discussion around how change affects governance, stakeholder perceptions and finance
weaknesses: The concept of âchangeâ is not woven into every analytical paragraph, leading to occasional generic CSR discussion
Introduction could more explicitly frame how âchangeâ drives the competitiveness analysis
Final statements restate findings without directly tying back to the key concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work firmly in band 4 (strong understanding with partial integration). The student consistently applies âchangeâ to Patagoniaâs re-structure, but the concept does not appear in every section. A score of 4/5 reflects strong, though not fully pervasive, integration.

strengths: Five core source documents are clearly numbered and cover Patagoniaâs profitability and market growth
Relevant external market data (Statista) provides sufficient basis for ratio calculations
weaknesses: Some screenshots contain promotional or unrelated content (e.g., Cyber Week promo) that does not support analysis
Inconsistencies in turnover figures (billions vs. millions) undermine depth
A few cited listings are tangential and dilute the focus of core evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are five documents and generally relevant data, uneven depth and inclusion of irrelevant/promotional material limit the quality. This aligns with band 2 (generally relevant but lacking consistent depth), meriting 2/4.

strengths: Use of SWOT, ratio analysis and an organisational-structure chart demonstrates basic tool selection
Each tool is applied to aspects of the research question (e.g., strengths/weaknesses from the new model, growth comparisons)
weaknesses: Selection is limited to a narrow toolkit; advanced frameworks (Porterâs Five Forces, Ansoff Matrix) are absent
Some ratio calculations are flawed or insufficiently explained
SWOT entries occasionally lapse into generic statements with unclear relevance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows more than superficial tool use but lacks breadth and rigor. It fits band 2 (some tools selected with superficial relevance), resulting in a 2/4 score.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Calculation of Patagoniaâs growth rate and comparison with industry averages is correctly executed
Threats section applies SWOT theory effectively, and opportunity analysis incorporates concrete industry examples
weaknesses: Discussion of data reliability, currency conversion and exchange-rate risks is missing
Analysis remains descriptive at times, with limited critical evaluation or alternative viewpoints
Units are mislabelled (billions vs. millions), undermining data accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is adequate, but analytical evaluation is uneven and often descriptive. This matches band 2 (limited analysis of the research question), so a score of 2/5 is justified.

strengths: The conclusion restates the research question and provides a direct answer
Findings are generally consistent with earlier sections
weaknesses: Evidence in the conclusion is thin and some figures are questionable
There is minimal discussion of data limitations or reliability
The conclusion lacks depth and critical reflection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the research question is explicitly addressed, the conclusion is superficial and does not fully integrate or critique the evidence. This falls in band 1 (superficial connection to evidence and question), meriting 1/3.

strengths: Report follows IB conventions with title page, contents, introduction, analysis, conclusion and appendices
Table of Contents supports logical flow
weaknesses: Some sections end abruptly (e.g., incomplete paragraph on the new structure)
Internal cross-references are missing or inconsistent (page numbers not provided)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure is present but marred by abrupt transitions and incomplete sections. This aligns with band 1 (limited structure), so a 1/2 score is appropriate.

strengths: All required presentation elements (title page, word count, research question, bibliography) are included
weaknesses: Table of Contents lacks page numbers for sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although all required elements are present, inconsistencies in citations and navigability issues place the work in band 1 (one or more required elements partially missing/underdeveloped), warranting 1/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows IB IA conventions: title page, table of contents, introduction, analysis, conclusion, references and appendices, enabling logical flow.  Minor lapses (e.g., abrupt section endings) exist, but overall organisation is appropriate, warranting the highest band for this criterion.

Most documents relate directly to Patagoniaâs structure, profitability and industry growth, providing sufficient depth for basic ratio calculations and SWOT evidence.  Nonetheless, some figures appear inconsistent (e.g., turnover in the tens of billions) and a few screenshots are promotional rather than analytical, limiting depth.  This aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ but not the highest level that requires both depth and breadth.
The IA includes the research question, word count, table of contents, citations, bibliography and labelled appendices/source documents, fulfilling all required presentation elements for band 2.

Five source items are listed in the âSource Documentsâ section (screenshots of web pages plus Statista chart), meeting the 3-5 requirement.  The student also cites additional websites in the Works Cited, but only the five labelled documents are treated as core evidence, matching the expectations for this band.

Each chosen tool is applied directly to the research question: the SWOT comments on strengths, weaknesses and threats arising from the new model; ratio analysis compares Patagoniaâs growth with market growth.  However, calculations are sometimes flawed or unexplained, and SWOT points occasionally drift into generic statements.  Application is therefore adequate but not consistently clear or rigorous, matching band 3.

The student selects revenue growth figures for Patagonia and the industry, plus limited profitability data, and performs simple percentage calculations.  While data choices are relevant, they are narrow and occasionally inconsistent, yet still adequate for a basic investigation.  This corresponds to âadequate selection and use of data.â

Analysis goes beyond description by comparing Patagoniaâs growth to industry averages and linking SWOT factors to competitiveness, but the reasoning is often superficial and lacks integration across sections.  Limited critical evaluation of data reliability or alternative viewpoints means the work shows only âlimited analysis of the research question,â fitting band 2.
The student explicitly restates the research question and provides a clear answer in the conclusion, satisfying the descriptor for band 3.

The tools chosenâSWOT, ratio/percentage growth calculation and a brief organisational-structure chartâconstitute an adequate but not extensive toolkit.  They are appropriate for assessing competitiveness yet lack more advanced frameworks (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces, Ansoff) that would demonstrate a wider, more effective selection, placing the work in the âadequate selectionâ category.

The conclusionâthat Patagonia has become more competitiveâis generally aligned with earlier findings, but supporting evidence is thin and some figures are questionable.  Thus consistency is partial rather than full, meeting the band 2 descriptor.

Patagonia is the sole focus throughout and organisational details are accurate (private ownership, Purpose Trust, Holdfast Collective, revenue figures).  The link between the change in ownership structure and competitive position is analysed via SWOT points and ratio commentary, demonstrating solid integration into the assessment.  Nevertheless, the analysis is occasionally surface-level (e.g., generic claims about CSR attracting customers) and not sustained across every section, matching the descriptor of âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ rather than comprehensive, fully-integrated coverage.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the key concept of change, describing the 2022 ownership transfer and the prior 2018 mission-statement shift, and links these events to competitiveness.  Explanations show a clear grasp of why the re-structure represents organisational change and how it might influence stakeholder perceptions, governance and finance.  However, the concept is not woven into every analytical paragraph; at times discussion drifts into generic CSR benefits without explicitly tying back to "change."  This fulfils the descriptor of âstrong understanding with partial integration,â but falls short of the pervasive, fully-integrated treatment required for the top band.

annotations.comment: Threats section comprehensively covers risks, showing effective application of SWOT theory.
Identifying âChangeâ as the key topic aligns well with the investigationâs focus on organizational shifts.
All required presentation elements (title page, word count, session, student number) are present, enhancing compliance.
The research question is clearly articulated, providing a focused inquiry into Patagoniaâs business model change and competitiveness.
Final statement reaffirms answer, but incorporating the concept of âchangeâ directly would strengthen integration.
Source Document 2 includes promotional content (âCYBER WEEK PROMOâ) irrelevant to analysis; crop or remove extraneous sections.
Source Document 3 table is rich in data, but discussion on its reliability or context (e.g., benefit corp status) is missing.
Additional US listings information is unrelated to Patagonia and should be excluded to maintain relevance.
Statista market forecast document notes currency conversion; include discussion of exchangeârate risks in analysis.
The table of contents is organized but lacks page numbers for each section; adding them will improve navigability.
The Table of Contents structure supports logical flow and reflects appropriate organization throughout the report.
Background context is provided, but the introduction could more explicitly state how the change concept frames the competitiveness analysis.
The introduction outlines industry context but should specify the metrics and tools that will assess competitiveness more clearly.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Restating the research question at the end of the introduction reinforces focus and clarity.
The description of Patagoniaâs original model provides useful context, but lacks explicit analysis linking back to the concept of change.
Introducing the business hierarchy chart is an effective tool selection to visualize structure.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The analysis of familial decision-making power is insightful and applies governance theory effectively.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The paragraph ends abruptly (âIt then châ); review and complete the discussion of the new structure.
Consider adding a comparative diagram of the new ownership model to reinforce analysis of change.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: SWOT structure is clear, demonstrating an adequate selection of analytical tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: SWOT factors are described but often lack quantitative evidence or direct linkage to Patagoniaâs data.
The opportunity section begins but needs full development of potential growth scenarios.
Opportunity analysis later incorporates specific industry examples, enriching depth and relevance.
The rationale for ratio analysis is stated, but it would benefit from justification of chosen financial indicators.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Claiming CSR drives customer attraction requires academic or empirical references to support the assertion.
Turnover figures are mislabelled in billions instead of millions; correct units to ensure data accuracy.
Contextual explanation of the significance of market outperformance aids interpretation of ratio results.
External market growth rate is correctly sourced from Statista, demonstrating relevance of supporting documents.
Mathematical workings are clear, but formatting with LaTeX $$...$$ would align with IB mathematical presentation.
The student effectively computes Patagoniaâs growth rate, showing application of percentage change calculations.
Interpretation that Patagonia outperforms the market growth is sound, linking results to competitiveness.
The six-year time span is justified, but discuss potential limitations of using a single timeframe.
The conclusion summarizes findings, but could more explicitly discuss data reliability and limitations.
The Works Cited list is comprehensive, but consistency in citation style (italics, punctuation) should be checked.
The Source Documents are relevant and well-numbered, demonstrating appropriate depth of core evidence.
